Patch Set 8: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(10 inline comments)

There are a few style issues to be fixed.

Also, a number of improvements are labeled (performance), but since urgent OI scrub performance is critical to being able to use the filesystem we may want to address these in the same Lustre release as when OI scrub is landed, instead of waiting until Phase IV of this project.

Finally, two potential defects are present that definitely prevent this patch from landing as-is.

Patch Set 8:

Unfortunately, while I had made draft comments on the first version of this patch in I584c2873cfb11bd488bc60ba175842c71fb03664, I didn't complete the review, and these comments were sitting there for a long time.

Patch Set 18: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

sorry, I don't find this reimplementation good. I'm not against *proper*  code re-usage, but here you call to whole bunch of code just to re-use few lines (AFAICS). and you mix two different behaviors inside osd_scrub_inode_iteration().

if you want to re-use some code structure it properly please.
Uploaded patch set 19.

Update patch as Alex suggestion.

Patch Set 19: Verified

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.2/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/21473798-b345-11e1-a6d7-52540035b04c).  Ran 12 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 19: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

(1 inline comment)

honestly, I still think the code is more complicated than it should be for this functionality. Andreas, any thoughts?
Patch Set 19: (1 inline comment)
Patch Set 19: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 19: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(7 inline comments)

I agree that this code might be restructured to be less complex, but I don't think it is worthwhile to do that until we have more idea about what requirements the later phases of the code might have.  There are a couple of other minor issues that should be fixed before landing, however.

My thought while looking at this code was to move the iteration entirely into osd_iit_next() so that it handled loading of bitmaps (stored in the "scrub" context) and was only passed the "*pos" and "*dev" as arguments.  That would allow simplifying the callers instead of having the inode iteration multiplex between the functions.

Another option would be to have a callback context that is passed by the caller and run for each inode (calling the "next" and "exec" functions), rather than having osd_inode_iteration() check "if (preload == 0)" each time.

The reason I do NOT ask for this to be restructured again right now, is that I'd rather stick with something working right now, and only change the code if there is a requirement in later phases to do so.  I'm OK with the cleanup that has been done so far, and want to keep moving these patches along.

parents.subject: LU-1513 libcfs: call cfs_alloc with proper masks

commit.subject: LU-957 scrub: osd-ldiskfs itable based iteration
commit.message:
LU-957 scrub: osd-ldiskfs itable based iteration

Implement inode table based object iteration in osd-ldiskfs.
It is implemented as DT iteration APIs, which can be used by
up layer LFSCK to scan the whole device sequentially.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <yong.fan@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I3d80bb4a174d47429764e5cca35e4f07be52d50b
